Creating Prelaunch Videos:
Part One
Module 4
Screencap video vs. Full Motion video
• Screencap is often easier than Full Motion
• Create a presentation
• Turn on microphone and start talking
• With Full Motion I’d have to have a video
setup (background)
• Get lights & camera
• I’d have to care about how I looked (get
dressed up, etc.)
Screencap video vs. Full Motion video
• It’s easier to deliver a script with Screencap
• This isn’t scripted - but I’d want to script my
Prelaunch Content
• Even if it’s just bullet points
• If this is your first launch you probably want to
script it too
• If you’re going to deliver from a script, a
screen capture video is a lot easier to do
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 	2
Screencap video vs. Full Motion video
• With screencap, you usually record the audio first
and then do the on-screen video recording
• So you’re doing just one step at a time
• Some people don’t like to be on camera… Full
Motion is painful
• The reality is – you should start getting in front
of the camera and start practicing
Screencap video vs. Full Motion video
• It’s generally easier for the viewer to connect with
you with Full Motion
• It’s easier to edit screencap after the fact
• If I make a mistake I can edit it and no one
would know on a screencap
• If you have to edit a Full Motion video – it’s
often easier to add the edit in as a screencap
Screencap video vs. Full Motion video
• Screencap can often be a lot faster to create
• Screencap can be slower if you want to use fancy
animations
• The fancier you make it, the longer it takes to
create
• You can mix Full Motion with Screencap
• I’ve done this a lot in my last several launches
• (Full motion intro, then we’ll go to screen
capture for teaching)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
